methods.method_name: Sediment Sample Preparation and ICP-MS Analysis
methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting sediment samples, preparing them through digestion with Aqua Regia, and analyzing the resulting solutions for elemental composition using Inductively Coupled Plasma - Mass Spectrometry (ICP-MS). It also includes quality control steps using a certified reference material.

steps.description: Sediment samples were collected from each site in continuous manner by depth.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples continuously by depth from multiple sites

steps.description: Selected aliquots of sediment from late Holocene sites were transferred to pre-weighed digestion vessels.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer selected sediment aliquots to pre-weighed digestion vessels

steps.description: All solutions were prepared with certified trace-metal grade HNO 3 (67-70% w/w) and HCl (36% w/w) and ultra-pure (18MΩ) water.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare acid and ultra-pure water solutions for digestion

steps.description: digested with Aqua Regia (3:1 HCl:HNO 3 mol/mol) in Savillex PFA containers
steps.searchable_summary: Digest homogenized sediment with Aqua Regia in PFA containers

steps.description: heated at 90 °C for 1 hour on a heating block.
steps.searchable_summary: Heat the digestion mixture at 90°C for 1 hour

steps.description: After cooling, the solutions were then diluted with 18 MΩ water
After cooling, the solutions were then diluted with 18 MΩ water
steps.searchable_summary: Dilute the cooled digested solutions with 18 MΩ water
Analyze the diluted solutions by ICP-MS

steps.description: The certified reference material (SARM-7, SACCRM) was digested using the same procedure as a means of corroboration.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest certified reference material (SARM-7) using the same procedure as samples for quality control

steps.description: The value of the certified reference material (SARM-7, SACCRM) was 3.74 ± 0.05 ppm and the measured value was 4.27 ± 0.13 ppm.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare measured value of certified reference material to certified value

steps.description: ICP-MS analyses were completed on a Thermo Scientific X Series II instrument.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ICP-MS analysis using a Thermo Scientific X Series II instrument
inputs: Thermo Scientific X Series II ICP-MS instrument

steps.description: A peristaltic pump using a Cetac ASX 520 auto-sampler pumped sample solutions.
steps.searchable_summary: Pump sample solutions using a peristaltic pump and auto-sampler

steps.description: The internal standard was added in-line using a Trident Internal Standard Kit.
steps.searchable_summary: Add internal standard in-line during ICP-MS analysis

steps.description: The sample was introduced into the plasma using a MicroMist EzyFit nebulizer, which reduced oxide formation with a high total dissolved solids tolerance, and reduced the sample uptake rates.
steps.searchable_summary: Introduce sample into ICP-MS plasma using a MicroMist EzyFit nebulizer

steps.description: The cyclonic spray chamber was kept at 3 °C, minimizing oxide formation.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain cyclonic spray chamber temperature at 3°C

steps.description: Ion lens voltages, nebulizer flow, and stage positioning were optimized every 24 hours using a tuning solution to maximize the ion signal and stability and minimize oxide levels (CeO + /Ce + ) and doubly charged ions (Ba 2+ /Ba + ).
steps.searchable_summary: Optimize ICP-MS instrument parameters (ion lens, nebulizer flow, stage positioning) using a tuning solution

steps.description: A calibration check of the standards was analyzed following initial calibration, at the end of the sample run, and after every 12 samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform calibration checks of standards periodically during ICP-MS run

steps.description: All data generated or analyzed during this study are included in this published article (and its Supplementary Information files).
steps.searchable_summary: Access data from published article and supplementary information files
